title: 1.1: Tests that HealObject restores an object when its data is removed from a disk
1.2: Tests that HealObject repairs an object when its metadata has an outdated modification time
1.3: Tests that unreferenced data parts are removed during the healing process
1.4: Tests that HealBucket recreates a bucket when it is missing from a disk
1.5: Tests that HealObject restores a versioned object when disk data is missing
1.6: Tests that HealObject repairs versioned objects with outdated metadata
1.7: Tests that delete markers created under quorum loss are healed
1.8: Tests that objects created under quorum loss are healed with updated version count
1.9: Tests that deleting a specific version under quorum loss is healed with updated version count
1.10: Tests that multipart objects are healed when metadata is missing from parity disks
1.12: Tests that HealObject restores an object when its xl.meta file is deleted
1.13: Tests that HealObject repairs an object when its xl.meta file contains corrupted data
1.14: Tests that HealObject returns an error when metadata is missing beyond read quorum
2.1: Places the test for basic healing scenarios in the cmd directory
2.2: Places the test for versioned healing scenarios in the cmd directory
2.3: Places the test for dangling object handling in the cmd directory
2.4: Places the test for multipart object healing in the cmd directory
2.5: Places the test for corrupted metadata healing in the cmd directory
3.1: All tests use the Go testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the Test<Name> naming convention
3.3: All tests use the context.WithCancel pattern with deferred cancellation
3.4: All tests use t.Fatal or t.Fatalf for critical failures
4.1: Tests initialize erasure-coded storage with multiple disks
4.2: Tests clean up temporary disk directories after completion
